scenario.name: streaming-concurrency-sweep
scenario.description: WebSocket streaming concurrency sweep c=1..256 on T4 GPU. T4 16GB VRAM limits max viable concurrency to ~1-16 streams; c=32+ causes VRAM exhaustion failures.

quality.normalization: Whisper EnglishTextNormalizer (lowercase, expand contractions, strip punctuation)

performance.notes: T4 16GB VRAM constrains streaming to low concurrency. c=1 is the only level with 0 failures. c=32 had 51.5% failure rate. c=64+ had 100% failure. Peak throughput at c=32 was 10.97x RTFx / 77.6 sess/min but with 103 failures — not production-viable.

reproduction.notes: T4 Turing architecture: torch.compile and CUDA graphs disabled via ConfigMap. max_concurrent_streams=256 in config but actual viable concurrency is ~1-16 due to 16GB VRAM. T4 is recommended for batch workloads; use L4 for high-concurrency streaming.

cost.price_source: GCP n1-standard-4 + T4 GPU on-demand reference price
cost.notes: Cost per audio hour not meaningful at c=1 (sub-realtime RTFx 0.78). T4 streaming is viable for low-volume single-stream use cases only. For cost-effective streaming, use L4.

artifacts.name: concurrency_sweep
artifacts.description: Full concurrency sweep results (c=1 to c=256)
